mnemonics: HIVE STORM SPARK! (Hadoop for storage, Storm for real-time, Spark for fast processing) - The big data tools are causing a 'HIVE STORM' of 'SPARK'-ing insights for IoT data.
keyPoints: Hadoop (HDFS & MapReduce) provides a distributed file system and batch processing framework for storing and processing vast amounts of IoT sensor data.
Apache Spark offers in-memory processing, making it significantly faster than Hadoop MapReduce for iterative algorithms and real-time analytics on data streams.
Apache Storm is a distributed real-time computation system designed for processing unbounded streams of data, ideal for live IoT data feeds.
commonMistakes: Assuming Hadoop is for real-time analytics; it is primarily designed for batch processing large datasets, not live streams.
Thinking Spark is just a faster version of Hadoop; it is a separate processing engine that can run standalone or on top of Hadoop for resource management (YARN).
Using a single tool for all jobs; the correct tool choice (e.g., Storm for real-time vs. Hadoop for batch) is critical for IoT application performance.
